calibration.calibration_note: With hazard_harm=0.02, avg episode ~182 steps (was ~18 with 0.5). Actual episode time higher.

items.title: MECH-100 Event-Auxiliary Contrastive Encoder Loss (hazard_harm=0.02, alpha=0.9)
items.description: FIXED: hazard_harm=0.02, alpha_world=0.9, warmup=1000. Tests SD-009/MECH-100: CE event classifier (Linear(world_dim,3)) backprops through z_world encoder. Labels: 0=none, 1=env_caused_hazard, 2=agent_caused_hazard. λ_event=0.5. PASS C1: calibration_gap>0.05, C2: event_classification_acc>0.5, C3: selectivity_margin>0.005.

items.title: Event-Auxiliary Contrastive Encoder Loss — Seed 1
items.description: Repeat of EXQ-020 with seed=1 for robustness.

items.title: Event-Auxiliary Contrastive Loss — Strong λ=2.0 (hazard_harm=0.02, alpha=0.9)
items.description: FIXED: hazard_harm=0.02, alpha_world=0.9, warmup=1000. EXQ-020 variant with λ_event=2.0 (4× stronger contrastive signal). If EXQ-020 achieves classification accuracy but not calibration_gap, tests whether stronger CE loss helps. Risk: forgetting of E1/E2 reconstruction.

items.title: MECH-104 Surprise-Gated Volatility Interrupt — surprise-proportional de-commitment selectivity
items.description: EXQ-061 (PASS) confirmed jitter mechanism is wired. EXQ-062 tests SELECTIVITY: does surprise-gated spike (|actual-predicted_harm| > threshold) de-commit less often than always-spike? Three conditions on single trained agent: (A) no interrupt — 0 uncommitted steps, (B) always-spike — frequent de-commitment, (C) surprise-gated — selective de-commitment. C4: n_uncommitted_C < n_uncommitted_B × 0.80.

items.title: SD-005 Latent Information Separation Probe — z_self action-selective, z_world event-selective
items.description: EXQ-047/047b FAIL via downstream task metrics (R²/attribution_gap insensitive at CausalGridWorld scale). Reframe: linear probes on frozen representations test information routing directly. SPLIT vs UNIFIED. Probe A: action decodability from z_self vs z_world. Probe B: event-type decodability from z_world vs z_self. PASS: double dissociation (C1/C2) + split more specialised than unified (C3/C4).
